title: Discrete-time hazard model with auxiliary remaining-laps target
summary: This target is naturally a one-step hazard: probability of pitting at lap t+1 given survival until lap t.
rationale: This target is naturally a one-step hazard: probability of pitting at lap t+1 given survival until lap t. Recent F1 pit-stop papers report strong gains from multi-tasking next-lap classification with remaining-laps-to-stop regression, and survival literature shows discrete-time hazard formulations often outperform plain binary framing for event timing.
implementation_hint: Keep the existing rows, but add an auxiliary label such as `laps_until_next_pit` within each `(Year,Race,Driver)` sequence. Train either a shared-backbone tabular NN or two-stage ensemble where model A predicts remaining laps and model B uses that OOF prediction plus current features to predict `PitNextLap`. Evaluate only with fold-safe auxiliary targets.
expected_effect: Better separation near pit windows, especially ambiguous medium-probability cases; likely small-to-moderate CV lift if current model underuses event-time structure.
risk: Auxiliary target engineering can leak if future pit locations are computed across fold boundaries or if final stints without future pits are mishandled.
disabled_reason: Disabled because an external duplicate-detection mechanism marked this hypothesis as duplicate of an existing hypothesis.
